steps.title: Spot the opening comma
steps.content: A comma after “that” marks the start of an interrupting phrase “through community involvement and scientific study.” The rest of the sentence must account for this opening punctuation.

steps.title: Provide the closing punctuation
steps.content:
Identify the structure: the sentence reads "efforts that, through community involvement and scientific [BLANK], aim to bring..." The phrase "through community involvement and scientific study" is parenthetical, opened by a comma after "that." The blank must close that parenthetical with a matching comma so the main clause ("aim to bring...") continues cleanly.
  • Choice A — study: "...that, through community involvement and scientific study aim to bring..." — the opening comma after "that" is never closed; the parenthetical runs into the verb. ✗
  • Choice B — study,: "...that, through community involvement and scientific study, aim to bring..." — closing comma matches the opening comma, correctly bracketing the parenthetical phrase. ✓
  • Choice C — study:: "...that, through community involvement and scientific study: aim to bring..." — a colon cannot close a parenthetical; it also incorrectly precedes a verb phrase. ✗
  • Choice D — study—: "...that, through community involvement and scientific study— aim to bring..." — an em dash cannot close a comma-opened parenthetical; mismatched punctuation. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
